name: Creme Brulee (Cook's Choice)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F and set rack in the middle level.
2. Place a shallow 1 1/2 quart baking dish in a larger pan, such as a roasting pan. Alternatively, use six individual molds.
3. Combine cream, sugar, and vanilla beans in a saucepan and bring to a boil, stirring once or twice to dissolve sugar.
4. Whisk the yolks in a bowl. Strain the cream mixture to remove the vanilla beans, then whisk the hot cream into the yolks. Skim the surface of the custard mixture and pour it into the mold.
5. Bake the creme about 1 hour until set but still very pale on the surface. Remove the mold from the pan of water and cool on a rack. Refrigerate several hours or overnight.
6. To caramelise the top of the Creme Brulee, unwrap and blot the surface with a paper towel. Sprinkle an even 1/16-inch layer of sugar on the surface. Run the mold under the preheated broiler (leave door open) or use a blow torch to caramalise the sugar. Serve as soon as the sugar hardens.
